paper.title_en: Cosmology from Nx2pt Analyses of SKAO Wide-Area Surveys
paper.abstract_en: SKAO surveys will provide an unprecedented window into the large-scale structure of the universe through HI 21cm galaxy and intensity mapping surveys, and radio continuum surveys. We present forecasts for the cosmological constraining power of ``$N\times2\mathrm{pt}$'' analyses -- which combine galaxy clustering, galaxy weak lensing, galaxy-galaxy lensing signals and 21cm Intensity Maps. By assuming cosmology surveys from an SKA-Mid AA4, we show that such an $N\times2\mathrm{pt}$ analysis will be able to deliver measurements of $\sim1\%$ precision on $Λ$CDM cosmological parameters. We also explore dynamical dark energy in the $w_0, w_a$ model, the sum of neutrino masses $\rm M_ν$, and the background curvature $Ω_{\rm k}$.
